records.ety: [F. content, fr. L. contentus, p. p. of contenire to hold together, restrain. See Contain.]

defs: Contained within limits; hence, having the desires limited by that which one has; not disposed to repine or grumble; satisfied; contented; at rest.
quotes: Having food and rai ment, let us be therewith content.

defs: That which is contained; the thing or things held by a receptacle or included within specified limits; as, the contents of a cask or bale or of a room; the contents of a book.
quotes: I shall prove these writings . . . authentic, and the contents true, and worthy of a divine original.

defs: Power of containing; capacity; extent; size.
Area or quantity of space or matter contained within certain limits; as, solid contents; superficial contents.
quotes: The geometrical content, figure, and situation of all the lands of a kingdom.

records.ety: [F. contenter, LL. contentare, fr. L. contentus, p. p. See Content, a.]

defs: To satisfy the desires of; to make easy in any situation; to appease or quiet; to gratify; to please.
quotes: Do not content yourselves with obscure and confused ideas, where clearer are to be attained.
Pilate, willing to content the people, released Barabbas unto them.

defs: To satisfy the expectations of; to pay; to requite.
quotes: Come the next Sabbath, and I will content you.

defs: Rest or quietness of the mind in one's present condition; freedom from discontent; satisfaction; contentment; moderate happiness.
quotes: Such is the fullness of my heart's content.
The sense they humbly take upon content.

defs: That which contents or satisfies; that which if attained would make one happy.
quotes: So will I in England work your grace's full content.

defs: An expression of assent to a bill or motion; an affirmative vote; also, a member who votes “Content.”.
quotes: Supposing the number of “Contents” and “Not contents” strictly equal in number and consequence.
